rows.description: Statuta. Dno Rectori Scholaribus et ff.
rows.notes: Top-of-page title/header. Ink and flourishes make several letters ambiguous (particularly the final word). No amounts in this line; interpreted as the page header.
Clear alignment with the three vertical ruling lines. Pounds '19', shillings '13' and pence '4' are legible and within valid ranges.
Numbers align clearly in the £/s/d columns: '26 10 0'. Handwriting of place-name ('Newington et Merton') is slightly stylized but readable.
Numeric columns are well aligned: '33 6 8'. The name reads as 'Shiers' though the final letters are slightly looped; chosen reading matches context.

rows.description: Augment: Comm: ejusdem Dno et Capelle
rows.notes: Clear numeric '60 0 0' in the £/s/d columns. Handwritten abbreviation 'Comm:' and 'ejusdem Dno et Capelle' are slightly compressed but legible.
Short description 'Do Rectori' and the numbers '30 9 2' are crisply written and perfectly aligned with the ruling lines.
Amounts '32 0 0' align well. Handwritten year and phrasing are readable though the word 'Annum' is slightly condensed.

Numerics '38 19 1' are clear, but the place and Latin abbreviation text are somewhat difficult to parse (I read 'Paddington pro 3 quartib. Anni'). Chose this reading based on letter shapes and context.
Repeated 'Do Rectori' line. Numeric '30 9 2' is clear and well-aligned.

rows.description: Stipend ad fest. Do Jo: Bapt: et Officiarios
rows.notes: Amount '19 0 0' is clear. The description contains several standard abbreviations ('Jo: Bapt:' for John the Baptist) and is slightly ornate but readable.
Numeric '33 6 8' is clear. The text is an abbreviated repetition ('Do - Dno Shiers'); letters are slightly stylized but consistent with earlier 'Shiers' entry.

rows.description: Augment: Comm: ejusd: Dno et Mod. et S.D.D.
rows.notes: Numbers '56 0 0' are clear. The descriptive text is congested and contains multiple abbreviations; several letters are uncertain, so I provided a cautious transcription ('ejusd:', 'Dno et Mod.' etc.).
Amount '12 19 8' is legible. The place-name appears as 'Hollington' (or 'Hollington'); chosen reading based on letter shapes. 'pro 4to parte Anni' is a standard phrase and readable.
Another clear 'Do Rectori' line with crisp numerals '30 9 2'.

rows.description: Stipend Gvinear ad festum St. Michaelis
rows.notes: Amounts '25 15 0' clearly aligned. The first word reads 'Gvinear' (or similar); context suggests a stipend entry. Word is slightly uncertain but digits are clear.
Numeric '28 12 5' is clear. The place name 'Wittenhame Longae' is my reading of the Latinized placename; letters are legible though somewhat compressed.
Final 'Do Rectori' in this block. '30 9 2' is clear and well aligned.

A subtotal/sum is visible at the right for the preceding block. The numerals read as '587 19 6' but the line is slightly faint and there is no accompanying text label; recorded as a total with moderate confidence.
New section header (Latin). The phrase 'Scholari Regis Caroli primi' is legible though the final 'primi' is written as '1mi' (typical abbreviation). No amounts on this header line.
Small amount at lower right reads '1 12 1' with a halfpenny loop after the pence (interpreted as 0.5). The fraction symbol is faint but shaped like the common 'ob' halfpenny mark.
Amount aligns as '8 10 10' with a small quarter-farthing-style mark after pence (interpreted as 0.25). The quarter mark is faint and a little ambiguous, hence moderate confidence.
Amount reads '2 13 9' with a three‑quarters-farthing style tail on the pence (interpreted as 0.75). The fraction mark is smudged but appears to be the '3q' style; moderate confidence.
Final faded total at the bottom right reads as '520 16 2 3/4'. The pence fraction is faint but resembles a '3q' (three farthings). The line is light and slightly smudged, so confidence is moderate.
